items.title: QuickBooks Online Login Issue Error? Here’s What to Do Today
items.author: goxely
items.content:
QuickBooks Online has become a trusted choice for businesses of all sizes, thanks to its user-friendly cloud-based platform. Designed to simplify tasks like managing finances, tracking expenses, creating invoices, and generating reports, it has made accounting easier for countless users. However, one issue that many encounter is trouble logging into their QuickBooks Online accounts. This article will explore the common reasons behind these login problems and offer practical solutions to overcome them.

Why Login Issues Happen in QuickBooks Online

If you’re unable to access your QuickBooks Online account, a few common causes might be to blame:

  1. - This is one of the most frequent reasons for login trouble. If you’ve forgotten your username or password, or if you’ve entered them incorrectly, you won’t be able to access your account.
  2. Browser Compatibility Issues - QuickBooks Online works best with certain browsers. Using an unsupported or outdated browser can lead to login problems.
  3. Cache and Cookies Build-Up - Your browser’s cache and cookies can sometimes store outdated or conflicting data, which might interfere with the login process.
  4. Unstable Internet Connection - A slow or unreliable internet connection can prevent you from successfully logging into QuickBooks Online.

How to Fix QuickBooks Online Login Problems

If you’re stuck with login issues, here are a few steps you can take to resolve the problem:

  1. Double-Check Your Login Information - Make sure you’ve entered the correct username and password. If you’ve forgotten your password, use the “Forgot password” option on the login page to reset it.
  2. - Clearing your browser’s cache and cookies will remove old data that might be causing login errors. This refreshes your browsing experience and can often fix the issue.
  3. - Ensure you’re using a browser that’s compatible with QuickBooks Online. Popular choices like Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Microsoft Edge work well with the platform.
  4. - If your browser is outdated, it might not function properly with QuickBooks Online. Updating it to the latest version ensures compatibility and improves performance.
  5. Check Your Internet Connection - Make sure your internet connection is stable and fast. Unstable or slow connections are common culprits behind login issues.
  6. Turn Off Browser Extensions - Certain browser extensions can interfere with how QuickBooks Online operates. Disable any extensions temporarily and try logging in again.
  7. Contact QuickBooks Support - If none of these solutions work, it’s time to reach out to QuickBooks Support. Their team can guide you through troubleshooting and help resolve any lingering issues.

Need Help? Contact QuickBooks Support

If you continue to face login issues, don’t hesitate to reach out for help. Call QuickBooks Customer Service at +1-855-838-5978 for prompt assistance.

Their experienced team is available to help you resolve any issues quickly and efficiently.

Struggling with QuickBooks Online login problems can be frustrating, but most issues are easy to resolve with a few simple steps. Whether it’s checking your credentials, clearing cache and cookies, or ensuring your browser is up to date, these solutions will help you regain access quickly.

If the issue persists, don’t hesitate to contact QuickBooks Support for expert assistance.

items.title: Today's GitHub Repo's
items.author: Dev Sk
items.content:
kagent, a Kubernetes-native framework, was designed for building, deploying, and managing AI agents within Kubernetes. It utilizes Kubernetes custom resources to simplify management and modification of the agents.

xlskubectl integrates Google Spreadsheets with Kubernetes, allowing users to administer their clusters from a spreadsheet using Kubernetes’ API to stream incremental updates.

git-who is a command-line tool that analyzes Git repositories to determine authorship of code components and subsystems. It provides three subcommands: table, which summarizes contributions by author; tree, which displays a file tree annotated with author information; and hist, which shows a timeline of commit activity.

items.title: 🔥 Gemma 3 27B vs. QwQ 32B vs. Deepseek R1 comparison ✅
items.author: Shrijal Acharya
items.content:
A few new open source models were released this March 2025, two of them being the QwQ 32B model from Alibaba and the new Gemma 3 27B model from Google, which are said to be good at reasoning. 🤔

Let’s see how these models compare against each other and against one of the finest open source reasoning models, the Deepseek R1 model.

And, if you’ve been reading my posts for quite some time, you know that I don’t agree with the benchmarks until I test them myself! 😉

If you want to jump straight to the conclusion, when compared against these three models, the answer is as the other blog posts, QwQ leads on coding but the other two models are again equally top notch on reasoning.

If it is for , go with the QwQ 32B model or Deepseek R1, or for anything else, Gemma 3 is equally awesome and should get your work done.

In the first week of March, Alibaba released this new model with a 32B model size, claiming it is capable of rivaling Deepseek R1, which has a model size of 671B. 🤯

This marks their initial step in scaling RL (Reinforcement Learning) to improve the model's reasoning capabilities.

Below is the benchmark they have made public to highlight QwQ-32B’s performance in comparison to another leading model, Deepseek R1.

Now, this is looking a lot more interesting, especially with the comparison they have done with a model that is about their size, Deepseek R1.

Brief on Gemma 3 27B Model

Gemma 3 is Google's new open-source model based on Gemini 2.0. The model comes in 4 different sizes (1B, 4B, 12B, and 27B), but that's not what's interesting.

It is said to be the "most capable model you can run on a single GPU or TPU". 🔥 This directly means that this model is capable of running on resource-limited devices.

It supports a with support for over languages, and it's mainly built for reasoning tasks.

However, the Gemma 3 27B model does not seem to perform as well on different coding benchmarks compared by many folks.

Let's see if that really is the case and how good this model is at reasoning.

💁 In this section, I will be testing the coding capabilities of all three of these models on an animation and a tough LeetCode question.

1. Rotating Sphere with Alphabets

Create a JavaScript simulation of a rotating 3D sphere made up of alphabets. The closest letters should be in a brighter color, while the ones farthest away should appear in gray color.

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Here's the output of the program:

The output we got from QwQ is completely insane. It is exactly everything I asked for, from the animation to the letters spinning to their colors changing. Everything is on point. So good!

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Here's the output of the program:

It does not seem to have completely followed my prompt. There definitely seems to be something happening, but I asked for a 3D sphere, and it seems that it got a ring spinning with the alphabets.

Knowing this model isn’t that good at coding, at least we got something working though!

Response from Deepseek R1

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Here's the output of the program:

It kind of got it correct as well and implemented exactly what I asked for. No doubt about that, but compared to the output from the QwQ 32B model, this really does not seem to compare in terms of overall output.

In this section, no doubt, QwQ 32B model is the winner. It really crushed both our coding tests with the animation and the hard LeetCode question. It does not quite seem to be fare that we are comparing these small models with Deepseek which is 671B (37B active per query) in model size, but quite surprisingly QwQ 32B beats Deepseek R1 here.

For this one, let’s do a quick LeetCode check with a super hard LeetCode question to see how these models handle solving a tricky LeetCode question with an acceptance rate of just 14.4%: Strong Password Checker.

Considering this is a tough LeetCode question, I have little to no hope with all three models as they are not as good as some other code models like Claude 3.7.

If you want to see how compares to some top models like and , check out this blog post:

Prompt:

A password is considered strong if the below conditions are all met:

- It has at least 6 characters and at most 20 characters.
- It contains at least one lowercase letter, at least one uppercase letter, and at least one digit.
- It does not contain three repeating characters in a row (i.e., "Baaabb0" is weak, but "Baaba0" is strong).

Given a string password, return the minimum number of steps required to make password strong. if password is already strong, return 0.

In one step, you can:

- Insert one character to password,
- Delete one character from password, or
- Replace one character of password with another character.

Example 1:

Input: password = "a"
Output: 5

Example 2:

Input: password = "aA1"
Output: 3

Example 3:

Input: password = "1337C0d3"
Output: 0

Constraints:

1 <= password.length <= 50
password consists of letters, digits, dot '.' or exclamation mark '!'.

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Damn, got the answer correctly. Not just that, it was able to write the entire code in time complexity, which is within the expected time complexity.

If I have to compare the code quality, I would say it is decent. Not just good code, it documented everything properly. So fair enough, the model seems to have a lot of potential.

Though it took a lot of time to think, it’s the working answer that really matters here.

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Okay, Gemma 3 falls short here. It got almost halfway there with test cases passing, but such faulty code does not even help. Better not to bother generating code at all than to code it poorly. 🤷‍♂️

But considering this model is an open-source model with just 27B parameters, which operates on a , that’s one thing we can consider.

Response from Deepseek R1

I have almost no hope for this model on this question. In my previous test, where I compared Deepseek R1 with Grok 3, Deepseek R1 failed pretty badly. If you’d like to take a look:

You can find the code it generated here: Link

Cool, it almost got it with test cases passing. But even a single test case failure will result in an incorrect submission, so yeah, hard luck here for Deepseek R1.

The result is pretty clear when comparing all three of these models on two coding questions. QwQ 32B model is clearly the winner 🔥, Gemma 3 27B did try but is definitely not something you would want to use for advanced coding. Can’t really say much on Deepseek, it’s but gets the job done for most basic to intermediate coding problems as I do use this model on a daily basis.

💁 Here, we will check the reasoning capabilities of both the models.

Let’s start off with a pretty simple question (not tricky at all) which requires a bit of common sense. But let’s see if these models have it.

I just wanted to test if the models can parse just what is asked and reason just what is needed or deal with everything that is given. Similar to asking what is ? 🥱

You start with 14 apples. Emma takes 3 but gives back 2. You drop 7 and pick up 4. Leo takes 4 and gives 5. You take 1 apple from Emma and trade it with Leo for 3 apples, then give those 3 to Emma, who hands you an apple and an orange. Zara takes your apple and gives you a pear. You trade the pear with Leo for an apple. Later, Zara trades an apple for an orange and swaps it with you for another apple. How many pears do you have? Answer me just what is asked.

As you can see, we provide all the unnecessary background with apples and oranges, but about Pear, which is just what is asked, it is at the end with one single trade, resulting in us having 0 pear.

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

Just as I thought, it seems to lack it completely. 😮‍💨 Seriously, it thought for 172 seconds () doing all the calculations of apples and oranges. This is definitely disappointing from QwQ 32B.

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

In just a few seconds, it was able to calculate all the situations and return the total pear count. Can't really complain much here.

The response was really super quick, completely impressed by this model.

Response from Deepseek R1

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

It thought for about a minute and did come up with the answer. It was expected that it would come up with the correct answer, but I just wanted to see if it could give me the answer to what I asked without doing all the unnecessary calculations with apples and oranges. Sadly, it failed as well.

To be honest, with this question, I wasn’t really looking for the correct answer; even a first grader could answer it. I was simply trying to see if these LLMs could filter out all the unnecessary details and answer just what is asked, but sadly, all of them failed, even though I added this sentence: "Answer me just what is asked." at the end of the prompt. 😮‍💨

A bald, skinny woman lives on the 78th floor of an apartment. On sunny days, she takes the elevator up to the 67th floor and walks the rest of the way. On rainy days, she takes the elevator straight up to her floor. Why does she take the elevator all the way to her floor on rainy days?

The question is somewhat tricky as I have included similar unnecessary details to distract the LLMs from easily finding the answer. The answer is that the woman is short and can't reach the button in the summer, but she carries an umbrella that she can use to press the elevator button higher.

The answer has nothing to do with the girl being bald or skinny. 🥴

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

It took a seriously long time here, 311 seconds (), and it took some time to figure out what that had to do with her being bald and skinny, but here, I am super impressed with the response.

The way it explained its thought process is really impressive. You may want to take a look as well.

Fair to say, QwQ 32B really got it correct and explained everything perfectly. ✅

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

I was really shocked by Gemma 3, within a couple of seconds, it got it correct. This model is looking solid on reasoning tasks. So far, I am super impressed with this Google open-source model. 🔥

Response from Deepseek R1

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

We know how good Deepseek is at reasoning tasks, so it's not really surprising that it got the answer correct.

It took some time to come up with the answer, thinking straight for about 72 seconds (), but I love the reasoning and thought process it comes up with every time.

It was really having a hard time understanding what the problem had to do with the woman being bald and skinny, but hey, that's the reason I added it. 🥱

There's no doubt that all three of these models are super good at reasoning questions. I especially love the way QwQ 32B and the Deepseek R1 model explain their thought process, and again, how quick Gemma 3 was at answering both questions. Solid 10/10 for all three models for getting to the answer ✅, but QwQ 32B sometimes might feel like it reasons unnecessarily. 🤷‍♂️

💁 Looking at the reasoning question answers from all three models, I was convinced that both of these models should also pass the math questions.

1. Clock Hands at Right Angle

At what time between 5.30 and 6 will the hands of a clock be at right angles?

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

Apart from the coding questions, Gemma 3 got this one correct as well and is crushing in all the reasoning and math questions I've mentioned. What a tiny yet powerful model this is.

Response from Deepseek R1

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

From this article of mine comparing Deepseek R1 and Grok 3, it is already clear how good Deepseek is at Mathematics, so I had high hopes for this model.

And as usual, it got this question correct as well. It definitely did a bit of long reasoning and thought process to come up with the answer, but it came up with the answer.

All three models are already doing so well with reasoning and mathematics. They all got it correct. Gemma 3 27B does it in no time, and the other two models, QwQ 32B and Deepseek R1, crush this as well with proper reasoning. ✅

In how many different ways can the letters of the word 'MATHEMATICS' be arranged so that the vowels always come together?

It’s a classic Mathematics question to ask an LLM, so let’s see how these three models perform.

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

After thinking for 552 seconds () 🥱, yes, it really took that long to come up with the answer, but as always, it nailed this question as well.

I agree that all its reasoning feels super long and boring, but if it gets the job done, then that's a good side to it. The QwQ 32B model is really looking solid and crushing all the questions so far. 🔥

Response from Gemma 3 27B

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

It was spot on. How quickly and accurately this model gives responses is just amazing. Google really did good work on this model, and there's no doubt about that. 😵

Response from Deepseek R1

You can find it's reasoning here: Link

After thinking for around 132 seconds (), it was able to come up with the answer, and once again, the correct answer from Deepseek R1.

The answer is obvious this time as well. All three of our models got it perfect with perfect explanation and reasoning. For a question this tough, it's a super impressive response to get back from all three models, and to me, it's Gemma 3 27B that just stands out. What a lightweight solid model. 🔥

The result is pretty clear. For me, after all this comparison, if I have to choose a model, it would still be Deepseek R1. The QwQ 32B model performed really well and can be said to be the clear winner of the comparison. ✅ The same seems to be the case with some other folks testing the models. 👇

But for me, Deepseek R1 model feels like a sweet spot with balanced reasoning and overall response time.

Even though Gemma 3 and Deepseek R1 couldn’t get the coding questions completely correct, they are just too good with overall reasoning. I can’t be more impressed with the Gemma 3 27B model. It really is a model you should have in your toolbox.

What do you think? Let me know your thoughts in the comments below! 👇

items.title: How AI is Revolutionizing Crypto Trading and Market Predictions
items.author: Kevin
items.content:
The cryptocurrency market is one of the most volatile financial ecosystems, making it both highly lucrative and incredibly unpredictable. Traditional trading strategies, which rely on human intuition and technical analysis, often struggle to keep up with the rapid fluctuations and emerging trends. This is where Artificial Intelligence (AI) steps in, offering traders powerful tools to enhance decision-making, automate trades, and predict market movements more accurately.

AI-driven trading systems analyze vast amounts of data in real-time, recognize patterns, and execute trades based on predefined algorithms. By integrating AI with crypto trading, traders and investors can reduce risks, optimize their portfolios, and gain deeper insights into market sentiment. In this blog, we will explore how AI is transforming crypto trading, its role in market predictions, and what the future holds for AI-powered trading strategies.

Understanding AI in Crypto Trading

Artificial Intelligence is reshaping the way crypto markets operate by bringing automation, precision, and data-driven insights into the trading space. Unlike human traders, AI can process large datasets, analyze price movements, and execute trades within milliseconds.

How AI is Used in Crypto Trading

  • Pattern Recognition: AI scans historical data to identify recurring trends and patterns that influence market fluctuations.

  • Real-time Data Processing: AI-powered algorithms analyze news, social media sentiment, and blockchain transactions to predict price movements.

  • Risk Management: AI helps traders manage risk by setting stop-loss and take-profit strategies based on market conditions.

  • Automated Trading: Trading bots execute buy and sell orders automatically, minimizing emotional biases and human errors.

By leveraging AI, traders can make more informed decisions while reducing manual effort and emotional biases in trading.

How AI Enhances Market Predictions

Predicting market movements in the crypto space is challenging due to the market’s high volatility. However, AI enhances accuracy in market forecasts by utilizing data science and machine learning techniques.

Key AI Methods for Crypto Market Predictions

  • Machine Learning Models: These models analyze historical price trends and trading volumes to predict future price movements.

  • Sentiment Analysis: AI scans social media platforms, news articles, and online discussions to gauge market sentiment and its potential impact on prices.

  • Predictive Analytics: AI-based tools generate price forecasts using statistical models and regression analysis.

AI-driven market predictions help traders anticipate price fluctuations, detect market cycles, and optimize entry and exit strategies.

AI-Powered Trading Bots & Automation

AI trading bots have become a game-changer in the crypto world, allowing traders to automate their strategies and execute trades 24/7 without human intervention.

  • Rule-Based Trading: Bots execute trades based on predefined conditions, such as moving averages and technical indicators.

  • High-Frequency Trading (HFT): AI bots analyze market conditions and execute multiple trades within seconds to capitalize on small price movements.

  • Arbitrage Trading: Bots identify price differences between exchanges and execute trades to exploit these gaps.

Although AI bots offer efficiency, traders must monitor them regularly to adjust strategies and avoid potential risks associated with market unpredictability.

AI & Blockchain Integration in Trading

The combination of AI and blockchain is revolutionizing crypto trading by enhancing security, transparency, and decentralization.

How Blockchain Supports AI in Trading

  • Decentralized Data Processing: AI-powered trading models utilize blockchain to ensure transparency and eliminate centralized control.

  • Smart Contracts: AI-driven smart contracts enable automated trade execution without the need for intermediaries.

  • On-Chain Data Analysis: AI analyzes real-time blockchain data to detect anomalies, track transactions, and predict price trends.

Blockchain ensures that AI-powered trading remains transparent and tamper-proof, reducing fraud and manipulation risks.

The Future of AI in Crypto Trading

The fusion of AI and crypto trading is still evolving, but the future looks promising. Upcoming advancements include:

  • AI-Driven Decentralized Finance (DeFi): AI will further optimize DeFi protocols, enhancing lending, staking, and liquidity management.

  • Personalized AI Trading Assistants: AI-powered assistants will offer real-time market insights tailored to individual traders.

  • Self-Learning Trading Bots: Future AI models will learn from market conditions and continuously improve their trading strategies.

  • AI-Powered Fraud Detection: AI will help identify and prevent suspicious trading activities, reducing fraud risks.

As AI continues to evolve, its integration with blockchain and crypto trading will create smarter, faster, and more efficient markets.

AI is revolutionizing crypto trading by bringing automation, predictive analytics, and intelligent decision-making into the industry. From algorithmic trading to sentiment analysis and portfolio optimization, AI is making trading more efficient and accessible. When combined with blockchain’s transparency and security, AI-driven crypto trading has the potential to reshape financial markets. As technology advances, we can expect AI to play an even greater role in driving innovation and efficiency in the crypto space.

items.title: Easily Build a UI for Your LangGraph AI Agent in Minutes with CopilotKit 🪁
items.author: Bonnie
items.content:
In this article, you will learn how to build an agent-native research canvas app that combines human-in-the-loop capabilities using Langraph, CopilotKit, and Tavily.

Before we jump in, here is what we will cover:

  • Building and visualizing a LangGraph AI agent using LangGraph Studio
  • Building UI for a LangGraph AI agent using CopilotKit

Here’s a preview of the application we’ll be building.

In simpler terms, AI Agents are autonomous software programs that can perform tasks, make decisions, and interact with their environment using artificial intelligence.

In this context, they are systems that can conduct research, process information, and engage with humans during execution to ensure reliability and trustworthiness⁠.

CopilotKit is a full-stack framework for building user-interactive agents and copilots. It enables your agents to take control of your application, communicate what it's doing, and generate a completely custom UI.

To fully understand this tutorial, you need to have a basic understanding of React or Next.js.

We'll also make use of the following:

  • Python - a popular programming language for building AI agents with LangGraph; make sure it is installed on your computer.
  • LangGraph - a framework for creating and deploying AI agents. It also helps to define the control flows and actions to be performed by the agent.
  • OpenAI API Key - to enable us to perform various tasks using the GPT models; for this tutorial, ensure you have access to the GPT-4 model.
  • Tavily AI - a search engine that enables AI agents to conduct research and access real-time knowledge within the application.
  • CopilotKit - an open-source copilot framework for building custom AI chatbots, in-app AI agents, and text areas.
  • Docker - a platform for developing, shipping, and running applications in containers.

Building and Visualizing a LangGraph AI agent

In this section, you will learn how to build and start a LangGraph agent using Docker and visualize its workflow using LangGraph Studio.

The repository contains two folders: the agent and the front end. To start the agent, navigate to the agent directory.

Then install all the agent dependencies using pip.

Next, create a  file in the agent directory. Then add OpenAI, Tavily, and LangSmith API keys into the environment variables.

If you open the file, it defines a MasterAgent class that conducts a research workflow.

It uses a directed graph (StateGraph) to manage states and transitions between the LangGraph AI agent nodes, tool execution, and human feedback.

The workflow is designed to assist in generating research reports by gathering data, proposing outlines, and writing sections—all while allowing human feedback via a front-end integration using CopilotKit.

To start the Langraph AI agent, open the Docker app and run the following command to start the agent.

Once the LangGraph API server has started, navigate to the LangGraph Studio using the LangGraph Studio link provided. Note the API URL from the output (e.g., http://localhost:8123). We will use it to connect the agent to the front end using CopilotKit Cloud.

After that, the LangGraph agent will open on the LangGraph studio, and you can visualize it, as shown below.

To test the LangGraph agent, add a message to the  state variable and click the Submit button.

The agent will then process the input by following the defined workflow through the connected nodes and respond to your message in a thread, as shown below.

Before we continue, let’s talk about a key concept in agentic copilots called Human-in-the-Loop (HITL). HITL allows agents to request human input or approval during execution in order to make AI systems reliable and trustworthy.

In this case, you can add HITL to the agent by clicking one of the nodes and marking the checkbox, as shown below.

Then add another message, such as “research about AI models,” to the  state variable and click the Submit button. The agent will start researching AI models, and once it is done, it will ask you to review the sections and provide your feedback or any specific changes you would like to make, as shown below.

Add a “yes” message to the  state variable and click the Submit button. The agent will process the message and provide you with the outline proposal for the report on AI models. Then it will ask you if you would like to approve the outline or if there are any changes you would like to make, as shown below.

Reply with the “I would like to approve the outline” message and click the submit button. Then the agent will compile a report on AI models grouped in different sections and finish the research process, as shown below.

Now that we have learned how to visualize and test a LangGraph AI agent using LangGraph Studio, let’s see how we can add a frontend UI to interact with it.

Building UI for LangGraph Agent using CopilotKit

In this section, you will learn how to connect your LangGraph AI agent to the CopilotKit frontend UI using the CopilotKit cloud.

Step 1: Create a tunnel for a LangGraph AI agent

To create a tunnel to the LangGraph AI agent, use the command below so Copilot Cloud can connect to it. Remember the API URL I told you to note when starting the agent; use the port number provided. In my case, the port number is 8123.

Select a project, and the tunnel should be live and connected to Copilot Cloud, as shown below.

Then navigate to the frontend folder.

After that, install the frontend dependencies using pnpm.

Then start the app using the command below.

Let’s now see how to build UI for the LangGraph AI agent using CopilotKit.

Step 2: Setup CopilotKit Provider

To set up the CopilotKit Provider, the  component must wrap the Copilot-aware parts of your application. For most use cases, it's appropriate to wrap the CopilotKit provider around the entire app, e.g., in your , as shown below in the frontend/src/app/layout.tsx file

Step 3: Choose a Copilot UI

To set up your Copilot UI, first import the default styles in your root component (typically ).

In this case, we will use CopilotChat defined in the file.

The chat component is then imported and used in the file. The chat is then rendered on the frontend UI, as shown below.

Step 4: Creating a shared state between your UI and LangGraph AI agent

CoAgents maintain a shared state that seamlessly connects your UI with the agent's execution. This shared state system allows you to:

  • Display the agent's current progress and intermediate results
  • Update the agent's state through UI interactions
  • React to state changes in real-time across your application

You can learn more about CoAgents’ shared state here on CopilotKit docs.

To create a shared state between your UI and LangGraph AI agent, first, you need to define the agent state and emit it to the front end, as shown in the file.

Then use the CopilotKit useCoAgent hook to share the LangGraph AI agent state with your frontend UI in the src/components/research-context.tsx file. The useCoAgent hook allows you to share state bidirectionally between your application and the agent.

 // Render child components within the provider

Next, render the agent's state in the chat UI. This is useful for informing the user about the agent's state in a more in-context way. To do this, you can use the useCoAgentStateRender hook in the file.

Then navigate to http://localhost:3000/, add “research AI models” to the chat, and press “Enter.” You should see the LangGraph AI agent state rendered in the chat UI, as shown below.

Step 5: Adding Human-in-the-loop breakpoint in your frontend UI

To allow the LangGraph agent to request human input or approval during execution in the chat UI, use the CopiloKit useCopilotKitAction hook named in the file.

Then navigate to http://localhost:3000/. Once the LangGraph agent is done researching about AI models, it will ask you to approve the proposal, as shown below.

Select the sections you want, add some remarks, and click the button. The LangGraph AI agent will start writing the research report on AI models.

Step 6: Streaming LangGraph AI agent Response in the UI

To stream the research report content, use the useStreamingContent hook defined in the src/lib/hooks/useStreamingContent.ts file in the file.

You should see the research content streaming on the right side, as shown below.

We covered a lot in this tutorial. I hope you learned how to build UI for an agentic copilot for your application with CopilotKit and also learned how to perform state changes in real-time and implement the human-in-the-loop concept.

Check out the full source code on GitHub here

Follow CopilotKit on Twitter and say hi, and if you'd like to build something cool, join the Discord community.

items.title: Better Patient Care Starts with Smarter Data Management
items.author: Himadri Patel
items.content:
The healthcare industry generates 30% of the world’s data, with this volume expected to grow at 36% annually by 2030. Yet, many healthcare providers struggle with data accessibility, interoperability, and security due to outdated infrastructure. Cloud computing is rapidly transforming how healthcare organizations store, manage, and access critical patient data—ensuring seamless operations and better patient care.

Why Does Healthcare Need Cloud Computing?

Traditional on-premise storage makes data retrieval slow and inefficient, leading to delayed treatments, redundant tests, and administrative burdens. With the rise of telemedicine, AI-powered diagnostics, and connected medical devices, a scalable, secure, and interoperable data solution is no longer optional—it’s essential.

How Cloud Computing Solves Healthcare Data Challenges

  1. Scalability & Efficiency: Cloud-based solutions like Azure Blob Storage & AWS S3 allow providers to handle large datasets seamlessly, improving data retrieval and storage flexibility.
  2. Interoperability: Implementing FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) and API integration ensures seamless data exchange between EHRs, diagnostic tools, and third-party applications.
  3. Secure Access & Compliance: IAM solutions, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and Zero-Trust frameworks ensure only authorized personnel access sensitive health records while maintaining HIPAA, GDPR, and HITECH compliance.
  4. Enhanced Data Quality: AI-driven frameworks using Azure Purview & AWS Glue eliminate duplicate records, validate real-time updates, and maintain structured patient histories.
  5. Data Protection: Encryption technologies like AWS KMS & Azure Storage Encryption safeguard patient information from cyber threats and unauthorized access.

Future-Proofing Healthcare with AQe Digital

At AQe Digital, we specialize in custom cloud-based healthcare solutions tailored for efficiency, security, and compliance. Our experts design scalable architectures, seamless integrations, and AI-powered insights to ensure healthcare providers are future-ready.

Instant access to patient records for better decision-making Secure, compliant data storage with encryption & monitoring

Optimized interoperability for seamless collaboration.

The future of healthcare is digital. Is your organization ready to unlock the power of cloud computing?

items.title: Build a Deep Research AI agent using Next.js, the Vercel AI SDK, and multiple LLMs, including Gemini and Deepseek.
items.author: CodeBucks
items.content:
I have built an open source Deep Research AI agent like Gemini or ChatGPT. Using Next.js, Vercel AI SDK, and Exa Search API, It generates follow-up questions, crafts optimal search queries, and compiles comprehensive research reports.

Using open router it is using multiple LLMs for different stages. At the last stage I have used gemini 2.0 reasoning model to generate comprehensive report based on the collected data from web search.

Let me know your thoughts, and feel free give star on the github repo.

items.title: How 3D Art Transforms the Gaming Experience
items.author: Vasundhara Infotech
items.content:
The gaming industry has transformed so widely, one of the major developments being the introduction and growth of 3D art. From its simple beginnings in 2D graphics, the development has thrived into wonderfully detailed, immersive three-dimensional environments across which viewers and players get globally captured.

Now, with 3D art, a game has rich living environments that you walk through from an open area or fight in a real-to-live battle arena. Then, you go through a very well-constructed cutscene with storytelling worthy of a corporation film. No longer will it be a 2-dimensional, pixelated world for the player to be put into when 3D art comes into play.

This blog will primarily discuss how 3D art revolutionizes gaming experiences and redefines gameplay, storytelling, and immersion in general. Everything from the details of character design to visual impact will be included in what makes up the different components of 3D art that have brought modern games to a new level of realism and excitement. Whether you are a gaming developer, designer, or a passionate gamer, knowing what 3D art can do for future appreciation will strengthen your appreciation for the rich visuals in which the 3D for games we play today are made.

3D art consists of the different technologies that need to fuse to bring an animated conversion of a scene or object into real life. The key representation of types of 3D art is as follows:

3D modeling uses special software to create a three-dimensional representation of a physical object or scenery. Models can be constructed from scratch using polygon modeling, spline modeling, or sculpting.

There are different kinds of 3D models:

Hard Surface Modeling: Rigged up and mechanical objects build (e.g. cars, weapons, buildings).

Organic Modeling: A modeling technique for soft, life-like shapes (such as the human form, animal shape, and plant form).

Low-poly Modeling: Models created with fewer polygons are usually used for performance-hungry games or mobile apps.

High-poly Modeling: This technique creates very finely detailed models for applications such as film or 3D printing.

Texturing is giving a 3D model color, material attributes, and reality by specifying surface details added to the models. Textures can simulate wood, metal, stone, or skin. Texture mapping is done through UV mapping, in which the model's surface is unwrapped into a 2-D space for easier painting or image mapping. Major types of textures include:

Diffuse/Color Map: Base color texture.

Normal Map: Add depth and detail to the surface without increasing polygons.

Specular Map: Controls shininess and reflectivity on a surface.

Bump Map: Simulates texture and surface detail but does not affect model geometry.

Displacement Map: Changes the model's geometry to give real depth or height.

Roughness Map: Determines surface smoothness or roughness for shading.

Lighting and shading are very important in representing a scene in 3D that lacks depth, mood, and realism. They characterize the model as it interacts with light, leading to a very different look. Lighting refers to all the light sources in a scene, such as where they are placed and what types they are. Types of lights comprise:

Point Light: Emits light from a single point in all directions.

Directional Light: Mimics sunlight, casting light in a specific direction.

Spotlight: Casts light in a cone shape, like a theater spotlight.

Ambient Light: Soft, even light that fills the scene.

Area Light: A light source with a specific area, average sources producing soft shadows.

Shading is the interaction of surfaces with light and how the materials look under different lighting conditions; nowadays, shaders simulating skin, metallic, glass, or cloth items control these.

Phong Shading: A simple shading model meant to produce smooth, shiny surfaces.

Blinn-Phong Shading: A variant of Phong shading that is more suitable for simulating certain kinds of reflections.

PBR (Physically-Based Rendering): A more advanced shading model that attempts to simulate the behavior of light on surfaces in the real world for better realism.

Rendering Finalization implements all the various 3-dimensional models, textures, light, and shade into a flat two-dimensional image or animation. There are several methods and engines for rendering.

Rasterization: Fast rendering technique exclusively used for real-time applications such as games. It ultimately produces an image through polygons, generating 3D models into two-dimensional images.

Ray-tracing: Ray-tracing performs more intensive computations while simulating how light behaves in an environment of objects. Thus, it produces high-quality reflections, refractions, and shadows.

Real-time Rendering: This refers to an application found in gaming and other interactive media with rendering capabilities on the fly that usually needs some optimization for performance.

Offline Rendering: Using this method renders movies and animations that do not have real-time rendering. Render times can be longer, but more complex calculations can be performed to arrive at photorealistic results.

Global Illumination: A rendering technique that simulates the bounce of light in a scene, effectively making it more realistic.

Such categories generally converge into a professional workflow where artists work simultaneously in more than one stage to complete a 3D project. In the end, this results in the combination of all these aspects, inducing a believable, interactive, or immersive 3D experience.

Uses of 3D Art in Interactive Entertainment In the realm of interactive entertainment, 3D art serves as the backbone of many game design elements, helping to create engaging and immersive worlds for players. Here are some key uses of 3D art Style in games:

In terms of interactive entertainment, 3D characters are necessary for making lifelike avatars and NPCs (non-playable characters) with enemies. They are designed, modeled, and textured as they interact with the players in video games, virtual reality (VR), and augmented reality (AR).

Visual effects (VFX) in an interactive entertainment medium create special effects that maximize the level of realism, drama, and excitement associated with the game or experience.

The environmental phenomena that are caused include fire or smoke, water, explosion, or magical effects physically related to them in a game: they probably constitute key gameplay or narrative elements for creating excitement or emotion.

VFX in gaming is to create visually stunning components, like a particle effect left behind after casting a spell, a weapon impact effect, or lighting elements that would create a realistic atmospheric condition. VFX helps make the immersive experience even more believable, so each player can have an environment that feels reactive to those actions and those about the direction of the story.

3D character creation involves sculpting minute specifications and creating textures resembling skin, clothing, and accessories, then assigning shaders that determine how the character reacts to light. Most of the time, these characters are involved in player interaction and become the vehicle for narrative, gameplay, and emotional involvement in the experience or game.

Well-designed 3D characters engage effectively to immerse the player's reality with the game world and connect with the digital environment.

If you talk about character animation, it actually brings to life all the 3D characters with very natural and expressive movements that counter the character action and work through the rigging. A setup where a character model has a skeletal body with animation applied via keyframes or motion capture data.

In games, character animation actually lends itself, for the most part, to be possible in actions like walking, running, jumping, or even fighting action and beyond, having emotional expressions like facial animations for dialogue or cutscenes.

3D Animation Style keeps these characters dynamic, adding real life to the game world. It creates a complete experience, a whole immersion in the game. Real-time animation creates possibilities of interaction through input from the player, thus giving the players a sense of agency in the world.

Visual effects (VFX) in an interactive entertainment medium create special effects that maximize the level of realism, drama, and excitement associated with the game or experience.

The environmental phenomena that are caused include fire or smoke, water, explosion, or magical effects physically related to them in a game: they probably constitute key gameplay or narrative elements for creating excitement or emotion.

VFX in gaming is to create visually stunning components, like a particle effect left behind after casting a spell, a weapon impact effect, or lighting elements that would create a realistic atmospheric condition. VFX helps make the immersive experience even more believable, so each player can have an environment that feels reactive to those actions and those about the direction of the story.

Environment design is the process of creating the spaces and worlds in which the game takes place. This includes designing landscapes, buildings, interiors, and other structural elements that players can explore.

3D modeling is essential in building these environments, which must feel aesthetically pleasing and functional within the game.

Detailed environments contribute to storytelling by setting the mood and tone of the game world. For example, a dark city of dystopia will have different emotional effects than a bright lush forest. Texturing, lighting, and atmospheric effects make these environments believable and interactive; this way, players may engage with and navigate through the world in a very realistic way.

Environmental design includes making interactive elements, such as open doors, traps that trigger, or objects that can be manipulated within the space.

Cinematics and Storytelling

Cinematics in interactive entertainment are pre-rendered or real-time animations used to advance the story and give cinematic experiences within games. These cutscenes help set the narrative tone, introduce important plot points, and show character development.

Real-time cinematics usually use in-game assets such as 3D characters, environments, and animations in modern games that can seamlessly blend gameplay and storytelling. Cinematics include anything from very short scenes in dialogue to fully movie-like moments that can greatly add emotions to the game.

As for 3D artwork, it significantly plays a part in interactive storytelling, where the background and characters change the scene according to the player's choices, making up a unique storyline. From here, storytelling intergrades with interactive gameplay to keep players more interested and closer to the game because they'd feel like an active member of the unfolding story itself.

Benefits of 3d Art in Games

The use of 3D art in interactive entertainment brings significant benefits that elevate both the player experience and the overall appeal of the game. Here are some key advantages:

Enhanced Realism and Immersion

As much as any other purpose, one of the most significant benefits of 3D artworks in games is an enriched realism and immersion therein. Through advanced techniques in 3D modeling, texturing, lighting, and shading, their artists create actual environments and characters that most players find appealing.

Realistic 3D visuals always help players immerse themselves in the game world, as if physically present in the game setting. Whether it be through intricately detailed textures of clothing a character might wear, reflections on body water surfaces, or whether light plays in shadows and shapes in a forest, visual realism entices all kinds of sensory experiences.

Improved Player Engagement

This is how the 3D graphics impact player engagement in different ways, making the game more dynamic and interactive, hence enjoying gameplay. Such games with high-quality graphics capture the players' attention with even improved effects, thus creating a visually stimulating environment for them.

Detailed character modeling, responsive animations, and interactive, vivid environments allow players to connect deeper with the game world. For example, the players will have a powerfully emotional investment in the fate of the 3D-rendered characters, have rich storylines through more realistic environments, and experience smooth, responsive movements during gameplay.

The donation of 3D art is likely to make a video game much more thriving and interesting. Generally, games that have very well-drawn-out 3D graphics and integrate all functionalities into the game are usually re-enjoyed by players over a certain time gap.

As technological changes occur from time to time, modifications of older 3D games by adding new textures, lighting, and animation techniques come to appeal to a very new audience or even bring back nostalgic players. The emergence of 3D environments and characters also allows for a complex and diverse kind of new evolved gameplay, where new areas, characters, and plot lines can be opened up, keeping the game fresh and exciting for the player.

In summary, 3D art enriches and elevates the overall experience of technically innovative games as it provides the needed realism to engage players again and again and abandons it with a much more lasting lure of the game world to maintain an increasingly large player base and keeps the game of interest-relevant after its initial launch.

Future Trends in 3D Game Art

The world of 3D gaming art is in rapid evolution as fueled by technological innovations and gamer increasing demands. One big trend, real-time ray tracing simulates the way light plays out with objects for making the lighting, reflections, and shadows incredibly realistic, thus it will increasingly grow more common as the hardware advances, greatly enriching the visual realism in the game.

Another exciting development is virtual reality and augmented reality, which are changing how players interact with 3D environments. VR games demand highly detailed, immersive 3D art that responds dynamically to player actions, while AR integrates the digital and physical worlds, offering new interactive possibilities.

Procedural generation is also becoming common in game design. That's a method of using an algorithm to generate game contents such as landscapes and other environments, and developers can go about creating huge, amazing worlds at one-tenth the time and cost relative to traditional approaches.

More fluid and responsive character animation with AI is another popular emerging trend; it simulates more responsive character interactions according to events in play. Rather than simply depending on keyframes or motion capture, AI brings real-time animation adjustment capabilities into the scene, thus greatly improving the dynamic feel of characters and objects.

Cloud gaming and high-quality game streaming platforms like Google Stadia are also changing how 3D art is delivered. With the power of cloud computing, developers can push the boundaries of 3D art by incorporating advanced visuals like real-time ray tracing without worrying about local hardware limitations.

Lastly, the future of 3D art in gaming will be impacted by user-generated content and increased customization. When the tools become even more accessible, players can generate their own 3D assets, characters, or even whole game worlds; games can have a much larger creative potential and allow highly personalized experiences.

The future of 3D game art is indeed exciting with the emergence of new trends, including real-time ray tracing, AI-driven animation, VR, AR, and procedural generation, changing how games look and feel. All these innovations enhance visual fidelity and make the player experience more immersive, interactive, and dynamic.

With the rapid pace of technological advancement, collaboration with experienced 3D art specialists ensures that developers will be ahead of the curve and deliver games that really captivate players with wonderful visuals and innovative gameplay. As 3D art continues to shape the future of interactive entertainment, partnerships with experts like Vasundhara Infotech form the key to creating games that push boundaries and give players unforgettable experiences.

Contact us today and learn how our 3D game development services are bringing your vision to life. Being one of the pioneering companies in 3D game development, we render solutions that are on top of the game.

items.title: The Case for Edge AI: Are Small Language Models the Future of AI?
items.author: Nadav Ben Itzhak
items.content:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have dominated AI discussions, but Small Language Models (SLMs) are quietly becoming a game-changer. They still significantly lag behind their larger counterparts in raw capability, research suggests they are catching up fast. Recent advancements in open-source models like Mistral-7B show that well-optimized smaller models can outperform larger ones in specific tasks. With improvements in efficiency, on-device processing, and fine-tuning, SLMs are becoming viable for real-world applications where size, speed, and privacy matter.

We came up with this for a client who was building an AI chat on top of a medical app that handled sensitive, HIPAA-compliant data. Their goal was to provide intelligent, on-device AI assistance while ensuring complete data privacy. By combining a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system with a small model running locally on a mobile device, they eliminated reliance on cloud-based processing. This setup allowed them to deliver real-time AI responses while keeping all patient information securely on the user’s device.

SLMs offer several key advantages over their massive cloud-based counterparts:

  • : Running a model locally eliminates the need for expensive API calls. Once deployed, inference is essentially free, making it ideal for cost-conscious businesses.
  • : Since SLMs run on local devices, sensitive data never leaves the user’s machine. This is crucial for industries like healthcare, finance, and defense.
  • : Without relying on cloud-based servers, SLMs can deliver faster responses, making them perfect for real-time applications like chatbots, automation tools, and voice assistants.
  • : Unlike cloud-reliant LLMs, SLMs can operate without an internet connection, making them useful for scenarios where connectivity is unreliable—think airplanes, submarines, or remote locations.
  • : SLMs can be fine-tuned for specific tasks without requiring the massive infrastructure needed for training large-scale models.

Use cases for SLMs span across industries—medical diagnostics, on-device personal assistants, airline systems, military applications, and embedded AI solutions in consumer devices.

The Challenge: Local Storage and Processing

The biggest challenge of deploying SLMs locally is balancing performance with device constraints. Even a relatively small 7B parameter model requires significant memory and processing power. Quantization and pruning techniques can reduce model size while preserving performance, but careful optimization is needed to ensure smooth real-world deployment.

Another key challenge is handling local storage. Traditional cloud-backed AI systems store and retrieve data externally, while SLMs must manage this data on-device efficiently. This is where lightweight, edge-optimized databases like come in.

The Experiment: Running an SLM Locally with GoatDB

This is a demo of a ChatGPT-like interface that runs completely in the browser, no network connection needed. It uses GoatDB for storing chat history, and wllama for running the models.

Currently, it runs the Stories15M model which generates fun stories but can easily be adapted to specific use cases.

  1. for local data storage.
  2. for text generation.
  3. to create a self-contained AI assistant.

The Results: Local AI Chat

The demo is deployed on a single AWS t4g.nano machine with 2 ARM VCPUs, 0.5GB RAM and 8GB EBS.

Before continuing, make sure you have Deno 2+ installed. If not, install it from here. Then, run the following commands inside your project's directory.

Small Language Models are proving to be a viable alternative to massive cloud-based AI systems. With zero inference costs, better privacy, and offline capabilities, they offer an attractive solution for edge computing applications. Tools like make local AI even more practical by handling data storage seamlessly. While challenges like memory constraints and inference speed remain, rapid advancements in model optimization are making SLMs more powerful than ever.

If you’re a developer looking to explore offline AI, now is the perfect time to experiment with SLMs. Check out the GoatDB GitHub and start building your own local AI-powered applications.

items.title: How to Fix QuickBooks Time Login Problems Quickly - Here’s What to Do
items.author: goxely
items.content:
This article delves into the seamless integration of QuickBooks Time with QuickBooks Online, explores common login issues and their remedies, and provides an in-depth understanding of its features.

QuickBooks Time stands as a comprehensive solution for tracking and managing employee hours, enabling businesses to meticulously oversee work schedules, streamline project management, and simplify payroll processes. Its user-friendly interface and extensive functionality render it an exemplary choice for businesses of all sizes.

Common Causes of QuickBooks Time Login Issues

Understanding why login problems occur is the first step toward solving them. Below are some of the most frequent reasons users experience access issues:

  1. Unstable Internet Connection: A reliable internet connection is essential for accessing QuickBooks Time. Poor or inconsistent connectivity can interrupt the login process.
  2. Browser Compatibility Problems: Outdated cached data, lingering cookies, or improper browser settings can create login obstacles.
  3. Incorrect Login Credentials: Mistyping usernames or passwords is one of the most common reasons for failed login attempts.
  4. Outdated or Incorrect URL: Using an old or incorrect URL to access QuickBooks Time can lead to login failures.
  5. : Problems like expired subscriptions, inactive accounts, or outdated credentials may also block access.

Solutions to Resolve QuickBooks Time Login Issues

Here are practical steps to fix login challenges and get back on track:

  1. Verify Your Login Credentials: Double-check your username and password for accuracy. If you’ve forgotten your credentials, use the “Forgot Password” option to reset them.
  2. Clear Browser Cache and Cookies: Old cached data and cookies can cause conflicts. Follow these steps to clear your browser history:
  • Open your browser (e.g., Chrome).
  • Click the three-dot menu in the top-right corner.
  • Select “Clear browsing data” or press Ctrl + Shift + Delete.
  • Choose a time range (e.g., “All time”) and select options like cached images and cookies.
  • Click “Clear Data” to finalize the process.

  1. Check Your Internet Connection: Make sure your internet connection is stable. If necessary, restart your router or modem to resolve connectivity issues.

  2. : Private browsing modes bypass cached data and can help resolve login issues. Shortcuts for incognito mode:

  • : Ctrl + Shift + P
  • : Command + Shift + N
  1. Use the Correct QuickBooks Time URL: Always log in via the official QuickBooks Time URL: tsheets.intuit.com. Avoid outdated or unofficial links.

Still stuck? QuickBooks customer support is just a call away at (855) 389-9729. They can guide you through the next steps.

How to Integrate QuickBooks Time with QuickBooks Online

Integrating QuickBooks Time with QuickBooks Online makes managing timesheets, invoices, and reports seamless. Here’s a step-by-step guide to set it up:

Find the QuickBooks Time App: In QuickBooks Online, search for “QuickBooks Time” under the Apps section.

: Click “Get App Now” and follow the prompts to begin the installation.

Set Time Tracking Preferences

  • In QuickBooks Online, go to the gear icon > Account and Settings > Time.
  • Configure settings, such as your workweek start day and the information displayed on timesheets.
  • Navigate to Get Paid & Time > Time in QuickBooks Online, then click Launch QuickBooks Time.

Sync Data Between Platforms

  • In QuickBooks Time, go to QuickBooks > Preferences and import customers, service items, and vendors from QuickBooks Online.
  • Choose the items you want to sync, then click Start Import.

Export Timesheets to QuickBooks Online

  • Once your team has tracked their time, export approved timesheets directly into QuickBooks Online.
  • Go to the QuickBooks icon in QuickBooks Time, select Export all approved time, and confirm.

The integration syncs data automatically every minute. For manual updates, navigate to QuickBooks > Import > Company Settings in QuickBooks Time

QuickBooks Time login is crucial for businesses that need real-time collaboration on financial data. However, connectivity issues, permission errors, or file corruption can cause disruptions. This guide outlines troubleshooting steps to resolve common multi-user mode issues. If problems persist, contact QuickBooks Customer Service at 855-389-9729 for expert assistance.

items.title: Understanding Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)
items.author: Victor Leung
items.content:
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) is a powerful machine learning technique that enhances the alignment of artificial intelligence (AI) systems with human preferences. By integrating human feedback into the training process, RLHF has become a cornerstone for fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 and Claude, enabling them to generate more accurate, helpful, and contextually appropriate outputs.

RLHF involves a three-phase process that combines supervised learning and reinforcement learning:

  1. : The model is pretrained on large-scale datasets using supervised learning objectives like next-word prediction. This phase establishes the model's foundational understanding of language and context.

  2. : A reward model is trained to evaluate the quality of the AI's outputs based on human feedback. Human annotators rank or score responses, providing a signal for what constitutes "good" or "bad" behavior. These rankings are used to train the reward model, which predicts scores for unseen outputs.

  3. Reinforcement Learning Fine-Tuning: Using reinforcement learning techniques—most commonly Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)—the language model is fine-tuned to optimize its outputs according to the reward model's guidance. This iterative process ensures that the AI aligns more closely with human preferences over time.

Key Challenges and Limitations of RLHF

Despite its effectiveness, RLHF faces several challenges that can limit its performance and scalability:

  1. Subjectivity in Human Feedback: Human preferences are diverse and context-dependent, leading to inconsistencies in feedback. Annotators may unintentionally introduce biases or errors due to fatigue or personal perspectives.

  2. : If the training data or human feedback contains biases, these can be reinforced during the RLHF process, potentially leading to harmful or unfair outputs.

  3. Reward Model Misalignment: The reward model may fail to capture complex human preferences accurately, leading to "reward hacking," where the AI optimizes for superficial metrics rather than genuine understanding.

  4. : Over-optimization during RLHF can reduce diversity in responses, as the model tends to prioritize high-scoring but repetitive outputs over creative or varied ones.

  5. : RLHF is resource-intensive, requiring significant computational power for training large models and handling complex dataflows across multiple GPUs.

  6. Adversarial Vulnerabilities: RLHF-trained models remain susceptible to adversarial attacks that exploit weaknesses in their safeguards, potentially causing them to generate harmful or unintended content.

Examples of RLHF Implementations

Several prominent AI systems have successfully implemented RLHF:

  • : GPT-4 was fine-tuned using RLHF to improve its conversational abilities while adhering to ethical guidelines. Human feedback helps refine its capacity for producing accurate and safe responses.

  • : Anthropic employs RLHF alongside principles-based alignment techniques to ensure its models prioritize helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness in their outputs.

  • : Gemini integrates RLHF into its training pipeline to enhance its generative capabilities while aligning with user expectations and safety standards.

Future Directions for RLHF

To address current limitations and unlock the full potential of RLHF, researchers are exploring several promising directions:

  1. : Developing more sophisticated reward models capable of capturing nuanced human preferences will reduce issues like reward hacking and misalignment.

  2. Efficient Training Techniques: Optimizing resource allocation and leveraging techniques like distributed training can help mitigate the high computational costs associated with RLHF.

  3. Robustness Against Bias and Adversarial Attacks: Incorporating methods like adversarial training and fairness-aware feedback mechanisms can enhance the safety and reliability of RLHF-trained models.

  4. Scalability Across Domains: Expanding RLHF beyond conversational AI into areas like code generation, mathematical reasoning, or multimodal tasks will broaden its applicability.

Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback has revolutionized how AI systems align with human values and expectations. By combining human intuition with advanced reinforcement learning algorithms, RLHF ensures that large language models generate outputs that are not only accurate but also aligned with ethical standards. However, addressing its limitations—such as bias amplification, computational inefficiencies, and adversarial vulnerabilities—will be critical for advancing this technique further. With ongoing research and innovation, RLHF holds immense potential for shaping safer and more effective AI systems across diverse applications.

items.title: Vibe Coders and the Last 20%
items.author: LunarDrift
items.content:
In the field of AI, achieving 80% accuracy is relatively easy, but the final 20% is exceptionally difficult. As a developer, I deeply understand the impact of this "80/20 rule" in real-world projects. Whether it's autonomous driving or AI programming, improving precision often requires far more effort than anticipated. Take autonomous driving as an example: despite significant technological advancements over the past few decades, fully reliable self-driving cars remain out of reach. Even the most advanced systems cannot completely avoid errors in extreme scenarios. This "last mile" problem is not only a technical challenge but also a test of developers' patience and attention to detail.

In 2023, Roose, in his coverage of chatbots, underestimated the severity of the "hallucination" issue. Recent studies show that even advanced models like GPT-4.5 still produce outputs with "hallucinations" in over one-third of standardized tests. As a developer, I am well aware of the complexity behind this phenomenon. AI still has fundamental limitations in understanding and generating content, especially when dealing with ambiguous or open-ended problems, where error rates rise significantly. While AI-generated code can quickly accomplish basic tasks, its error rate in complex scenarios is equally concerning. These errors may persist for decades, particularly when building innovative applications. The precision issues in AI programming not only affect development efficiency but also pose potential security risks.

Writing code is just the first step; debugging and maintenance are the real challenges. For non-programmers, fixing AI-generated code is nearly impossible. For instance, if the "Lunchbox Buddy" image recognition system malfunctions, users could face significant frustration. In scenarios like automated email writing, code errors could lead to serious consequences. Non-programmers struggle to ensure the stable operation of these systems, let alone resolve complex issues like backend network calls. Roose's article overlooks the difficulties of debugging and maintenance, making his perspective overly optimistic and even resembling commercial promotion. As a developer, my experience with tools like Cursor AI is mixed. While they can improve development efficiency, the actual workload for debugging and maintenance often far exceeds expectations. Roose's examples are too simplistic to reflect the real challenges of AI programming. The effort required to debug and maintain complex systems is much greater than he suggests.

Many parents and educators may discourage children from learning to code, believing that AI can already replace human developers. However, the reality is that large-scale projects still require experienced software architects, and this demand will not change for decades. In 2016, Geoff Hinton predicted that deep learning would surpass radiologists within five years. Yet, nine years later, radiologists have not been replaced; instead, many regions face a shortage of them. This case reminds us that the practical application of AI technology is far more complex than theoretical predictions. As tech writer Mayo Olshin pointed out, "If we blindly trust AI's output due to a lack of expertise, the long-term harm will far outweigh the short-term productivity gains." This insight profoundly reveals the risks of over-reliance on AI.

As a developer, I believe the advancements in AI are undoubtedly exciting, but we cannot ignore the challenges of improving precision and the complexities of debugging and maintenance. Blind optimism and hype not only mislead the public but could also hinder the healthy development of the technology. In practice, developers—especially "Vibe Coders"—value flexibility and creativity in their work. AI-generated code often falls short in meeting these needs. Vibe Coders prefer manual debugging and optimization to ensure code quality and performance, rather than relying on automated tools. While this approach is time-consuming, it better addresses complex scenarios and personalized requirements. For Vibe Coders, the human touch in coding remains irreplaceable, and AI is seen more as a supplement than a replacement.

items.title: The AI efficiency framework from Pruna AI is now open-source
items.author: Bertrand Charpentier
items.content:
I am Bertrand from Pruna AI. Together with John, Rayan, Stephan, we created Pruna AI to tackle challenges in AI model optimization. We’re a group of researchers in AI efficiency and reliability, originally from TUM.

Since we got so many times questions on how compression of AI models was working under the hood, we decided to open-source the package with the help of all the Pruna AI team. As a whole, the package is an AI efficiency framework that can be installed with to compress models, and thus save memory and compute power when running AI models for inference.

With open-sourcing, people can now inspect and contribute to the open code. Beyond the code, we provide detailed readme, tutorials, benchmarks, and documentation (https://docs.pruna.ai/en/stable/index.html) to make transparent compression, evaluation, and saving/loading/serving of AI models.

Beyond the open-source package, we commercially offer with advanced compression methods, recovery methods, and an optimization agent to unlock greater efficiency and productivity gains.

We are pleased to share this with you all. We would be glad to hear your thoughts and questions in the comments :)

items.title: What Are the Essential Features of an eCommerce Website?
items.author: Shikharsparx
items.content:
In today’s digital-first world, having a robust eCommerce website is no longer optional—it’s a necessity. Whether you’re a small business or a large enterprise, your online store is often the first point of contact for customers. To stand out in a competitive market, your eCommerce website must be user-friendly, secure, and feature-rich. But what exactly makes an eCommerce website effective? In this article, we’ll explore the essential features of an eCommerce website and how partnering with a eCommerce website development company can help you build a platform that drives sales and enhances customer satisfaction.

## Why eCommerce Website Features Matter

The success of an online store depends on its ability to provide a seamless shopping experience. From intuitive navigation to secure payment options, every feature plays a crucial role in converting visitors into customers. A well-designed eCommerce website not only boosts sales but also builds trust and loyalty among users. This is why businesses often turn to a custom eCommerce development company to create tailored solutions that meet their unique needs.

## Essential Features of an eCommerce Website

Here are the must-have features that every eCommerce website should include:

  • Ensure that customers can easily find products with clear categories, filters, and search functionality.
  • With the majority of online shoppers using mobile devices, a mobile-friendly design is non-negotiable. This also ties into mobile app development cost if you plan to extend your platform to a dedicated app.
  • Optimize your website for speed to reduce bounce rates and improve user experience.

2. High-Quality Product Pages

  • Provide comprehensive information about each product, including specifications, features, and benefits.
  • Use multiple high-quality images and videos to showcase products from different angles.
  • Display user-generated reviews and ratings to build trust and credibility.

3. Secure Payment Gateways

  • Multiple Payment Options: Offer a variety of payment methods, including credit/debit cards, digital wallets, and buy-now-pay-later options.
  • Ensure that all transactions are secure with SSL certificates and PCI compliance.
  • Display all costs, including taxes and shipping fees, upfront to avoid cart abandonment.

4. Advanced Search and Filtering

  • Implement an auto-suggest search bar to help customers find products quickly.
  • Allow users to filter products by price, size, color, brand, and other relevant attributes.

5. Personalized Shopping Experience

  • Use AI-driven algorithms to suggest products based on browsing history and preferences.
  • Wishlists and Saved Carts: Enable customers to save items for later or create wishlists for future purchases.

6. Efficient Checkout Process

  • Allow customers to make purchases without creating an account.
  • Simplify the checkout process for returning customers.
  • Provide real-time updates on order status and shipping details.

7. Robust Security Features

  • Two-Factor Authentication: Add an extra layer of security for user accounts.
  • Implement tools to detect and prevent fraudulent transactions.
  • Conduct periodic audits to identify and fix vulnerabilities.

8. Scalability and Performance

  • Ensure your website can handle traffic spikes with scalable cloud hosting solutions.
  • Content Delivery Network (CDN): Use a CDN to deliver content faster to users across the globe.
  • Optimize your database to ensure smooth performance even during peak times.

9. SEO and Marketing Tools

  • Use clean, keyword-rich URLs to improve search engine rankings.
  • Publish relevant content to drive organic traffic and engage customers.
  • Email Marketing Integration: Automate email campaigns for abandoned carts, promotions, and newsletters.

10. Customer Support Features

  • Offer real-time assistance to address customer queries and concerns.
  • Provide answers to common questions to reduce support requests.
  • Easy Returns and Refunds: Simplify the return process to enhance customer satisfaction.

## The Role of a Custom eCommerce Development Company

Building an eCommerce website with all these features requires expertise and experience. This is where a custom eCommerce development company comes in. Here’s how they can help:

A professional company can create a customized eCommerce platform that aligns with your business goals and target audience.

From selecting the right artificial intelligence tech stack to implementing advanced security measures, a development company brings the technical know-how needed to build a robust website.

From planning and design to development and deployment, a custom eCommerce development company provides comprehensive support to ensure a seamless launch.

Post-launch, they offer maintenance and updates to keep your website running smoothly and securely.

An eCommerce website is more than just an online store—it’s a powerful tool for driving sales, building brand loyalty, and enhancing customer satisfaction. By incorporating essential features like user-friendly design, secure payment gateways, and personalized shopping experiences, you can create a platform that stands out in a competitive market. Partnering with a eCommerce website development company ensures that your website is not only feature-rich but also scalable, secure, and optimized for performance. Whether you’re launching a new online store or upgrading an existing one, investing in the right features and expertise can set you up for long-term success. With the right strategy, your eCommerce website can become a cornerstone of your business growth.
